title: Ordered CatBoost on raw identities plus cross features
summary: The dataset has very high-cardinality `Driver` and composite identities.
rationale: The dataset has very high-cardinality `Driver` and composite identities. CatBoost’s ordered scheme is explicitly designed to reduce target leakage from target statistics, which is relevant because naive same-fold encodings on repeated lap rows can overstate local CV.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoostClassifier with raw categorical columns plus composites like `Driver+Compound`, `Year+Race`, `Race+Stint+Compound`, and keep numerical rolling features. Use grouped folds by race/weekend and compare ordered boosting against current frequency-encoding-only trees.
expected_effect: Higher-value use of categorical history than simple frequency counts, potentially capturing driver- and track-specific pit tendencies without leaking label frequency.
risk: If folds are not grouped correctly, gains may be illusory; CatBoost can also underperform if numeric engineered features already dominate.
